name: Fillable

unions.name: Empty
unions.comment:
Like `Maybe`, but able to know at type-level whether `Empty` is possible.

    import Fillable exposing (Empty, filled)

    [ filled 1, filled 7 ]
        --: List (Empty never_ number_)
        |> List.map filling
    --> [ 1, 7 ]

[`Fillable.Empty`](#Empty) alone probably won't be that useful,
but it can make data structures type-safely non-emptiable:

    import Fillable exposing (filling)

    top : Empty Never (StackFilled element) -> element

    Fillable.map Dict.NonEmpty.head
    --: Empty possiblyOrNever (NonEmptyDict comparable v)
    --: -> Empty possiblyOrNever ( comparable, v ) possiblyOrNever

Go take a look at all the data structures in this package.

values.name: adaptType
values.comment:
Change the `possiblyOrNever` type.


#### Returning a type declaration value or argument that is `Empty Possibly`

An `Empty possiblyOrNever` can't be used as `Empty Possibly`

    import Fillable exposing (Empty)
    import Possibly exposing (Possibly)
    import Stack exposing (StackFilled)

    type alias Log =
        Empty Possibly (StackFilled String)

    fromStack : Empty possiblyOrNever_ (StackFilled String) -> Log
    fromStack stackFilled =
        stackFilled
            --: `possiblyOrNever_` but we need `Possibly`
            |> Fillable.adaptType (always Possible)


#### An argument or a type declaration value is `Empty Never`

The `Empty Never` can't be unified with `Possibly` or a type variable

    import Fillable exposing (Empty)
    import Stack exposing (StackFilled)

    theShorter :
        Empty Never (StackFilled element)
        -> Empty possiblyOrNever (StackFilled element)
        -> Empty possiblyOrNever (StackFilled element)
    theShorter aStack bStack =
        if Stack.length bStack > Stack.length aStack then
            bStack

        else
            --: `Never` but we need `possiblyOrNever`
            aStack

to make both branches return `possiblyOrNever`, use

    aStack |> Fillable.adaptType never
values.type: (possiblyOrNever -> possiblyOrNeverAdapted) -> Fillable.Empty possiblyOrNever filling -> Fillable.Empty possiblyOrNeverAdapted filling

values.name: andThen
values.comment:
Chain together operations that may return [`empty`](#empty).

If the argument [`Fillable.Empty`](#Empty) `Never` [`Empty`](Fillable#Empty),
a given function takes its [`filling`](#filling)
and returns a new possibly [`empty`](#empty) value.

Some call it `flatMap`.

    emptiableString
        |> Fillable.andThen parse
        |> Fillable.andThen extraValidation

    parse : ( Char, String ) -> Empty Possibly Parsed
    extraValidation : Parsed -> Empty Possibly Parsed
values.type: (filling -> Fillable.Empty possiblyOrNever fillingThen) -> Fillable.Empty possiblyOrNever filling -> Fillable.Empty possiblyOrNever fillingThen

values.name: empty
values.comment:
Insert joke about life here.

    empty
        |> Fillable.map (\x -> x / 0)
    --> empty
values.type: Fillable.Empty Possibly.Possibly filling_

values.name: filled
values.comment:
A [`Fillable.Empty`](#Empty) that certainly exists, allowing type-safe extraction.

    import Fillable exposing (filled, filling)

    filled "Bami" |> filling
    --> "Bami"
values.type: filling -> Fillable.Empty never_ filling

values.name: filling
values.comment:
Safely extracts the value from an `Empty Never`.

    import Fillable exposing (Empty, filled, filling)

    filled (filled (filled "Bami"))
        |> filling
        |> filling
        |> filling
    --> "Bami"

    first : Empty Never ( first, others_ ) -> first
    first =
        filling >> Tuple.first
values.type: Fillable.Empty Basics.Never filling -> filling

values.name: fromMaybe
values.comment: Convert a `Maybe` to a [`Fillable.Empty`](#Empty).
values.type: Maybe.Maybe value -> Fillable.Empty Possibly.Possibly value

values.name: ifFilled
values.comment:
If we have a `Filled`, alter the value using the [`filling`](#filling).

    import Fillable exposing (filled, ifFilled)
    import Stack exposing (addOnTop)

    Fillable.empty
        |> ifFilled addOnTop (filled 3)
        |> ifFilled addOnTop Fillable.empty
    --> Stack.only 3
    --: Empty Possibly (StackFilled number_)

If you dislike the argument positions, this might be for you:

    import Fillable exposing (filled, ifFilled)
    import Stack exposing (addOnTop)

    Fillable.empty
        |> (filled 3 |> ifFilled addOnTop)
        |> (Fillable.empty |> ifFilled addOnTop)
    --> Stack.only 3
    --: Empty Possibly (StackFilled number_)
values.type: (filling -> value -> value) -> Fillable.Empty possiblyOrNever_ filling -> value -> value

values.name: map
values.comment:
Change the possibly `Filled` value based on its current value:

    import Fillable exposing (filled)

    filled -3 |> Fillable.map abs
    --> filled 3

    Fillable.empty |> Fillable.map abs
    --> Fillable.empty
values.type: (filling -> fillingMapped) -> Fillable.Empty possiblyOrNever filling -> Fillable.Empty possiblyOrNever fillingMapped

values.name: map2
values.comment:
If all the arguments exist, combine them using a given function.

    import Fillable exposing (filled, empty)

    Fillable.map2 (+) (filled 3) (filled 4) --> filled 7
    Fillable.map2 (+) (filled 3) empty --> empty
    Fillable.map2 (+) empty (filled 4) --> empty
values.type: (aFilling -> bFilling -> fillingCombined) -> Fillable.Empty possiblyOrNever aFilling -> Fillable.Empty possiblyOrNever bFilling -> Fillable.Empty possiblyOrNever fillingCombined

values.name: toFillingOrIfEmpty
values.comment:
Lazily use a fallback value if the [`Fillable.Empty`](#Empty) [`empty`](#empty).

    import Possibly exposing (Possibly(..))
    import Fillable exposing (toFillingOrIfEmpty)
    import Dict

    Dict.empty
        |> Dict.get "Hannah"
        |> Fillable.fromMaybe
        |> toFillingOrIfEmpty (\_ -> "unknown")
    --> "unknown"

    filling =
        toFillingOrIfEmpty never
values.type: (possiblyOrNever -> filling) -> Fillable.Empty possiblyOrNever filling -> filling

values.name: toMaybe
values.comment:
Convert to a `Maybe`.

Don't try to use this prematurely.
Keeping type information as long as possible is always a win.
values.type: Fillable.Empty possiblyOrNever_ filling -> Maybe.Maybe filling

name: FocusList

unions.name: ListFocusingHole
unions.comment:
A special kind of list with elements of type `item`.

The focus can be

  - `🍍 🍓 <🍊> 🍉 🍇`: `ListFocusingHole Never`

  - `🍍 🍓 <?> 🍉 🍇`: `ListFocusingHole` [`Possibly`](https://dark.elm.dmy.fr/packages/lue-bird/elm-allowable-state/latest/Possibly)

    `<?>` means both are possible:

      - `🍍 🍓 <> 🍉 🍇`: a hole between items ... Heh.
      - `🍍 🍓 <🍊> 🍉 🍇`


#### in arguments

    empty : ListFocusingHole Possibly item_


#### in types

    type alias Model =
        WithoutConstructorFunction
            { choice : ListFocusingHole Never Option
            }

where

    type alias WithoutConstructorFunction record =
        record

stops the compiler from creating a positional constructor function for `Model`.

values.name: adaptHoleType
values.comment:
Change the `possiblyOrNever` type.

  - A `ListFocusingHole possiblyOrNever`
    can't be used as a `ListFocusingHole Possibly`?

        import Possibly exposing (Possible)

        FocusList.adaptType (always Possible)

  - A `ListFocusingHole Never`
    can't be unified with `ListFocusingHole Possibly` or `possiblyOrNever`?

        FocusList.adaptType never

Please read more at [`Fillable.adaptType`](Fillable#adaptType).
values.type: (possiblyOrNever -> possiblyOrNeverAdapted) -> FocusList.ListFocusingHole possiblyOrNever item -> FocusList.ListFocusingHole possiblyOrNeverAdapted item

values.name: after
values.comment:
The items after the location of the focus.

```monospace
🍍 🍓 <🍊> (🍉 🍇
```

    import Fillable exposing (Empty, filled)
    import Stack exposing (StackFilled, topAndBelow)

    FocusList.only 0
        |> FocusList.append [ 1, 2, 3 ]
        |> FocusList.next
        |> Fillable.map FocusList.after
    --> filled (topAndBelow 2 [ 3 ])
    --: Empty Possibly (Empty Possibly (StackFilled number_))
values.type: FocusList.ListFocusingHole possiblyOrNever_ item -> Fillable.Empty Possibly.Possibly (Stack.StackFilled item)

values.name: afterLast
values.comment:
Focus the hole after the end. Into the nothingness.

```monospace
🍓 <🍊> 🍉  ->  🍓 🍊 🍉 <>
```

    import Stack exposing (topAndBelow)

    FocusList.only 1                 -- <1>
        |> FocusList.append [ 2, 3 ] -- <1> 2 3
        |> FocusList.afterLast       -- 1 2 3 <>
        |> FocusList.plug 4          -- 1 2 3 <4>
        |> FocusList.toStack
    --> topAndBelow 1 [ 2, 3, 4 ]
values.type: FocusList.ListFocusingHole possiblyOrNever_ item -> FocusList.ListFocusingHole Possibly.Possibly item

values.name: alterAfter
values.comment:
Apply a function to all items coming after the current focussed location.

    import Stack exposing (topAndBelow)

    FocusList.only "zeroth"
        |> FocusList.append [ "first", "second" ]
        |> FocusList.alterAfter String.toUpper
        |> FocusList.toStack
    --> topAndBelow "zeroth" [ "FIRST", "SECOND" ]
values.type: (item -> item) -> FocusList.ListFocusingHole possiblyOrNever item -> FocusList.ListFocusingHole possiblyOrNever item

values.name: alterBefore
values.comment:
Apply a function to all items coming before the current focussed location.

    import Stack exposing (topAndBelow)

    FocusList.only "second"
        |> FocusList.prepend [ "zeroth", "first" ]
        |> FocusList.alterBefore String.toUpper
        |> FocusList.toStack
    --> topAndBelow "ZEROTH" [ "FIRST", "second" ]
values.type: (item -> item) -> FocusList.ListFocusingHole possiblyOrNever item -> FocusList.ListFocusingHole possiblyOrNever item

values.name: alterCurrent
values.comment:
If an _item_ is focussed, alter it based on its current value.

    import Stack exposing (topAndBelow)

    FocusList.only "first"
        |> FocusList.prepend [ "zeroth" ]
        |> FocusList.append [ "second", "third" ]
        |> FocusList.alterCurrent String.toUpper
        |> FocusList.toStack
    --> topAndBelow "zeroth" [ "FIRST", "second", "third" ]
values.type: (item -> item) -> FocusList.ListFocusingHole possiblyOrNever item -> FocusList.ListFocusingHole possiblyOrNever item

values.name: append
values.comment:
Put items to the end after anything else.

```monospace
              🍒🍋
🍓 <🍊> 🍉 🍇 ↓/
```

    import Stack exposing (topAndBelow)

    FocusList.only 123
        |> FocusList.append [ 456 ]
        |> FocusList.append [ 789, 0 ]
        |> FocusList.toStack
    --> topAndBelow 123 [ 456, 789, 0 ]
values.type: List.List item -> FocusList.ListFocusingHole possiblyOrNever item -> FocusList.ListFocusingHole possiblyOrNever item

values.name: appendStack
values.comment:
Put a [stack](Stack) of items to the end after anything else.

```monospace
              🍒🍋
🍓 <🍊> 🍉 🍇 ↓/
```

    import Stack exposing (topAndBelow)

    FocusList.only 123
        |> FocusList.append [ 456 ]
        |> FocusList.append [ 789, 0 ]
        |> FocusList.toStack
    --> topAndBelow 123 [ 456, 789, 0 ]
values.type: Fillable.Empty stackPossiblyOrNever_ (Stack.StackFilled item) -> FocusList.ListFocusingHole possiblyOrNever item -> FocusList.ListFocusingHole possiblyOrNever item

values.name: before
values.comment:
The items before the location of the focus.

```monospace
🍍 🍓) <🍊> 🍉 🍇
```

    import Fillable exposing (Empty, filled)
    import Stack exposing (StackFilled, topAndBelow)

    FocusList.only 0
        |> FocusList.append [ 1, 2, 3 ]
        |> FocusList.next
        |> Fillable.andThen FocusList.next
        |> Fillable.map FocusList.before
    --> filled (topAndBelow 0 [ 1 ])
    --: Empty Possibly (Empty Possibly (StackFilled number_))
values.type: FocusList.ListFocusingHole possiblyOrNever_ item -> Fillable.Empty Possibly.Possibly (Stack.StackFilled item)

values.name: beforeFirst
values.comment:
Focus the hole before the first item.
Remember that holes surround everything!

```monospace
🍍 🍓 <🍊> 🍉  ->  <> 🍍 🍓 🍊 🍉
```

    import Stack exposing (topAndBelow)

    FocusList.only 1                 -- <1>
        |> FocusList.append [ 3, 4 ] -- <1> 3 4
        |> FocusList.nextHole        -- 1 <> 3 4
        |> FocusList.plug 2          -- 1 <2> 3 4
        |> FocusList.beforeFirst     -- <> 1 2 3 4
        |> FocusList.plug 0          -- <0> 1 2 3 4
        |> FocusList.toStack
    --> topAndBelow 0 [ 1, 2, 3, 4 ]
values.type: FocusList.ListFocusingHole possiblyOrNever_ item -> FocusList.ListFocusingHole Possibly.Possibly item

values.name: current
values.comment:
The current focussed item.

```monospace
🍍 🍓 <🍊> 🍉 🍇  ->  🍊
```

    FocusList.only "hi there"
        |> FocusList.current
    --> "hi there"

    FocusList.only 1
        |> FocusList.append [ 2, 3, 4 ]
        |> FocusList.last
        |> FocusList.current
    --> 4
values.type: FocusList.ListFocusingHole Basics.Never item -> item

values.name: empty
values.comment:
An empty `FocusList` on a hole
with nothing before and after it.
It's the loneliest of all `FocusList`s.

```monospace
<>
```

    import Fillable

    FocusList.empty
        |> FocusList.toStack
    --> Fillable.empty
values.type: FocusList.ListFocusingHole Possibly.Possibly item_

values.name: findBackward
values.comment:
Find the first item matching a predicate, moving backwards
from the current position.

    import Fillable exposing (filled)

    FocusList.only 4
        |> FocusList.prepend [ 2, -1, 0, 3 ]
        |> FocusList.findBackward (\item -> item < 0)
        |> Fillable.map FocusList.current
    --> filled -1
values.type: (item -> Basics.Bool) -> FocusList.ListFocusingHole possiblyOrNever_ item -> Fillable.Empty Possibly.Possibly (FocusList.ListFocusingHole never_ item)

values.name: findForward
values.comment:
Find the first item in the `FocusList` the matches a predicate,
returning a `FocusList` focussed on that item if it was found.

This start from the current focussed location and searches towards the end.

    import Fillable exposing (filled)

    FocusList.only 4
        |> FocusList.append [ 2, -1, 0, 3 ]
        |> FocusList.findForward (\item -> item < 0)
        |> Fillable.map FocusList.current
    --> filled -1

    FocusList.only -4
        |> FocusList.append [ 2, -1, 0, 3 ]
        |> FocusList.findForward (\item -> item < 0)
        |> Fillable.map FocusList.current
    --> filled -4
values.type: (item -> Basics.Bool) -> FocusList.ListFocusingHole possiblyOrNever_ item -> Fillable.Empty Possibly.Possibly (FocusList.ListFocusingHole never_ item)

values.name: first
values.comment:
Focus the first item.

```monospace
🍍 🍓 <🍊> 🍉  ->  <🍍> 🍓 🍊 🍉
```

    FocusList.only 1
        |> FocusList.append [ 2, 3, 4 ]
        |> FocusList.prepend [ 4, 3, 2 ]
        |> FocusList.first
        |> FocusList.current
    --> 4
values.type: FocusList.ListFocusingHole possiblyOrNever item -> FocusList.ListFocusingHole possiblyOrNever item

values.name: focusingItem
values.comment:
[`Fillable.empty`](Fillable#empty) if the current focussed thing is a hole,
[`Fillable.filled`](Fillable#filled) if it is an item.

    import Fillable

    FocusList.only 3
        |> FocusList.append [ 2, 1 ]
        |> FocusList.nextHole
        |> FocusList.focusingItem
    --> Fillable.empty
values.type: FocusList.ListFocusingHole possiblyOrNever item -> Fillable.Empty possiblyOrNever (FocusList.ListFocusingHole never_ item)

values.name: insertAfter
values.comment:
Insert an item after the focussed location.

```monospace
        🍒
🍓 <🍊> ↓ 🍉 🍇
```

    import Stack  exposing (topAndBelow)

    FocusList.only 123
        |> FocusList.append [ 789 ]
        |> FocusList.insertAfter 456
        |> FocusList.toStack
    --> topAndBelow 123 [ 456, 789 ]

Insert multiple items using [`squeezeInAfter`](#squeezeInAfter).
values.type: item -> FocusList.ListFocusingHole possiblyOrNever item -> FocusList.ListFocusingHole possiblyOrNever item

values.name: insertBefore
values.comment:
Insert an item before the focussed location.

```monospace
      🍒
🍍 🍓 ↓ <🍊> 🍉
```

    import Stack exposing (topAndBelow)

    FocusList.only 123
        |> FocusList.insertBefore 456
        |> FocusList.toStack
    --> topAndBelow 456 [ 123 ]

Insert multiple items using [`squeezeInBefore`](#squeezeInBefore).
values.type: item -> FocusList.ListFocusingHole possiblyOrNever item -> FocusList.ListFocusingHole possiblyOrNever item

values.name: last
values.comment:
Focus the last item.

```monospace
🍓 <🍊> 🍉 🍇  ->  🍓 🍊 🍉 <🍇>
```

    import Stack exposing (topAndBelow)

    FocusList.only 1
        |> FocusList.append [ 2, 3, 4 ]
        |> FocusList.last
        |> FocusList.current
    --> 4

    FocusList.only 1
        |> FocusList.append [ 2, 3, 4 ]
        |> FocusList.last
        |> FocusList.before
    --> topAndBelow 1 [ 2, 3 ]
    --: Empty Possibly (StackFilled number_)
values.type: FocusList.ListFocusingHole possiblyOrNever item -> FocusList.ListFocusingHole possiblyOrNever item

values.name: map
values.comment:
Change every item based on its current value.

    import Stack exposing (topAndBelow)

    FocusList.only "first"
        |> FocusList.prepend [ "zeroth" ]
        |> FocusList.append [ "second", "third" ]
        |> FocusList.map String.toUpper
        |> FocusList.toStack
    --> topAndBelow "ZEROTH" [ "FIRST", "SECOND", "THIRD" ]
values.type: (item -> mappedItem) -> FocusList.ListFocusingHole possiblyOrNever item -> FocusList.ListFocusingHole possiblyOrNever mappedItem

values.name: mapParts
values.comment:
Apply multiple different functions on the parts of a `FocusList`- what
comes before, what comes after, and the current item if there is one.

    import Stack exposing (topAndBelow)

    FocusList.only "first"
        |> FocusList.append [ "second" ]
        |> FocusList.nextHole
        |> FocusList.plug "one-and-a-halfth"
        |> FocusList.mapParts
            { before = \item -> "before: " ++ item
            , current = \item -> "current: " ++ item
            , after = \item -> "after: " ++ item
            }
        |> FocusList.toStack
    --> topAndBelow
    -->     "before: first"
    -->     [ "current: one-and-a-halfth"
    -->     , "after: second"
    -->     ]
values.type: { before : item -> mappedItem, current : item -> mappedItem, after : item -> mappedItem } -> FocusList.ListFocusingHole possiblyOrNever item -> FocusList.ListFocusingHole possiblyOrNever mappedItem

values.name: next
values.comment:
Move the focus to the next item, if there is one.

```monospace
<🍊> 🍉 🍇  ->  🍊 <🍉> 🍇
```

    import Fillable exposing (filled)

    FocusList.only 0
        |> FocusList.append [ 1, 2, 3 ]
        |> FocusList.next
        |> Fillable.map FocusList.current
    --> filled 1

This also works from within holes:

```monospace
🍊 <> 🍉 🍇  ->  🍊 <🍉> 🍇
```

    import Fillable exposing (Empty, filled)

    FocusList.empty
        |> FocusList.insertAfter "foo"
        |> FocusList.next
    --> filled (FocusList.only "foo")
    --: Empty Possibly (ListFocusingHole Never String)

If there is no `next` item, the result is [`empty`](Fillable#empty).

    import Fillable

    FocusList.empty
        |> FocusList.next
    --> Fillable.empty

    FocusList.only 0
        |> FocusList.append [ 1, 2, 3 ]
        |> FocusList.last
        |> FocusList.next
    --> Fillable.empty
values.type: FocusList.ListFocusingHole possiblyOrNever_ item -> Fillable.Empty Possibly.Possibly (FocusList.ListFocusingHole never_ item)

values.name: nextHole
values.comment:
Move the focus to the hole right after the currently focussed location.
A hole is a whole lot of nothingness, so it's always there.

```monospace
🍍 <🍊> 🍉  ->  🍍 🍊 <> 🍉
```

    import Stack exposing (topAndBelow)

    FocusList.only "hello"
        |> FocusList.append [ "world" ]
        |> FocusList.nextHole
        |> FocusList.plug "holey"
        |> FocusList.toStack
    --> topAndBelow "hello" [ "holey", "world" ]
values.type: FocusList.ListFocusingHole Basics.Never item -> FocusList.ListFocusingHole Possibly.Possibly item

values.name: only
values.comment:
A `FocusList` with a single focussed item in it,
nothing before and after it.

```monospace
<🍊>
```

    import Stack

    FocusList.only "wat"
        |> FocusList.current
    --> "wat"

    FocusList.only "wat"
        |> FocusList.toStack
    --> Stack.only "wat"
values.type: element -> FocusList.ListFocusingHole never_ element

values.name: plug
values.comment:
Fill in or replace the focussed thing in the `FocusList`.

```monospace
🍓 <?> 🍉  ->  🍓 <🍒> 🍉
```

    import Stack exposing (topAndBelow)

    FocusList.empty
        |> FocusList.insertBefore "🍓" -- "🍓" <>
        |> FocusList.insertAfter "🍉"  -- "🍓" <> "🍉"
        |> FocusList.plug "🍒"         -- "🍓" <"🍒"> "🍉"
        |> FocusList.toStack
    --> topAndBelow "🍓" [ "🍒", "🍉" ]
values.type: item -> FocusList.ListFocusingHole possiblyOrNever_ item -> FocusList.ListFocusingHole never_ item

values.name: prepend
values.comment:
Put items to the beginning before anything else.

```monospace
🍒🍋
 \↓ 🍍 🍓 <🍊> 🍉
```

    import Stack exposing (topAndBelow)

    FocusList.only 1
        |> FocusList.append [ 2, 3, 4 ]
        |> FocusList.last
        |> FocusList.prepend [ 5, 6, 7 ]
        |> FocusList.toStack
    --> topAndBelow 5 [ 6, 7, 1, 2, 3, 4 ]
values.type: List.List item -> FocusList.ListFocusingHole possiblyOrNever item -> FocusList.ListFocusingHole possiblyOrNever item

values.name: prependStack
values.comment:
Put a [stack](Stack) of items to the beginning before anything else.

```monospace
🍒🍋
 \↓ 🍍 🍓 <🍊> 🍉
```

    import Stack exposing (topAndBelow)

    FocusList.only 1
        |> FocusList.append [ 2, 3, 4 ]
        |> FocusList.last
        |> FocusList.prependStack (topAndBelow 5 [ 6, 7 ])
        |> FocusList.toStack
    --> topAndBelow 5 [ 6, 7, 1, 2, 3, 4 ]
values.type: Fillable.Empty stackPossiblyOrNever_ (Stack.StackFilled item) -> FocusList.ListFocusingHole possiblyOrNever item -> FocusList.ListFocusingHole possiblyOrNever item

values.name: previous
values.comment:
Move the focus to the previous item, if there is one.

```monospace
🍊 <🍉> 🍇  ->  <🍊> 🍉 🍇
```

    import Fillable exposing (Empty, filled)

    FocusList.empty |> FocusList.previous
    --> Fillable.empty

    FocusList.only "hello"
        |> FocusList.append [ "holey", "world" ]
        |> FocusList.last
        |> FocusList.previous
        |> Fillable.map FocusList.current
    --> filled "holey"
    --: Empty Possibly (ListFocusingHole Never String)

This also works from within holes:

```monospace
🍊 🍉 <> 🍇  ->  🍊 <🍉> 🍇
```

    import Fillable exposing (Empty, filled)

    FocusList.empty
        |> FocusList.insertBefore "foo"
        |> FocusList.previous
    --> filled (FocusList.only "foo")
    --: Empty Possibly (ListFocusingHole Never String)
values.type: FocusList.ListFocusingHole possiblyOrNever_ item -> Fillable.Empty Possibly.Possibly (FocusList.ListFocusingHole never_ item)

values.name: previousHole
values.comment:
Move the focus to the hole right before the currently focussed location.
Feel free to [`plug`](#plug) that hole right up!

```monospace
🍍 <🍊> 🍉  ->  🍍 <> 🍊 🍉
```

    import Stack exposing (topAndBelow)

    FocusList.only "world"
        |> FocusList.previousHole
        |> FocusList.plug "hello"
        |> FocusList.toStack
    --> topAndBelow "hello" [ "world" ]
values.type: FocusList.ListFocusingHole Basics.Never item -> FocusList.ListFocusingHole Possibly.Possibly item

values.name: remove
values.comment:
Punch a hole by removing the focussed thing.

```monospace
🍓 <?> 🍉  ->  🍓 <> 🍉
```

    import Fillable exposing (filled)

    FocusList.only "hello"
        |> FocusList.append [ "holey", "world" ]
        |> FocusList.next
        |> Fillable.map FocusList.remove
        |> Fillable.map FocusList.toList
    --> filled [ "hello", "world" ]
values.type: FocusList.ListFocusingHole possiblyOrNever_ item -> FocusList.ListFocusingHole Possibly.Possibly item

values.name: squeezeInAfter
values.comment:
Append items directly after the focussed location.

```monospace
        🍒🍋
🍓 <🍊> \↓/ 🍉 🍇
```

    import Stack exposing (topAndBelow)

    FocusList.only 0
        |> FocusList.squeezeInAfter [ 4, 5 ]
        |> FocusList.squeezeInAfter [ 1, 2, 3 ]
        |> FocusList.toStack
    --> topAndBelow 0 [ 1, 2, 3, 4, 5 ]
values.type: List.List item -> FocusList.ListFocusingHole possiblyOrNever item -> FocusList.ListFocusingHole possiblyOrNever item

values.name: squeezeInBefore
values.comment:
Prepend items directly before the focussed location.

```monospace
      🍒🍋
🍍 🍓 \↓/ <🍊> 🍉
```

    import Stack exposing (topAndBelow)

    FocusList.only 0
        |> FocusList.squeezeInBefore [ -5, -4 ]
        |> FocusList.squeezeInBefore [ -3, -2, -1 ]
        |> FocusList.toStack
    --> topAndBelow -5 [ -4, -3, -2, -1, 0 ]
values.type: List.List item -> FocusList.ListFocusingHole possiblyOrNever item -> FocusList.ListFocusingHole possiblyOrNever item

values.name: squeezeStackInAfter
values.comment:
Append a [`Stack`](Stack) of items directly after the focussed location.

```monospace
        🍒🍋
🍓 <🍊> \↓/ 🍉 🍇
```

    import Stack exposing (topAndBelow)

    FocusList.only 0
        |> FocusList.squeezeStackInAfter
            (topAndBelow 4 [ 5 ])
        |> FocusList.squeezeStackInAfter
            (topAndBelow 1 [ 2, 3 ])
        |> FocusList.toStack
    --> topAndBelow 0 [ 1, 2, 3, 4, 5 ]
values.type: Fillable.Empty Possibly.Possibly (Stack.StackFilled item) -> FocusList.ListFocusingHole possiblyOrNever item -> FocusList.ListFocusingHole possiblyOrNever item

values.name: squeezeStackInBefore
values.comment:
Prepend a [`Stack`](Stack) of items directly before the focussed location.

```monospace
      🍒🍋
🍍 🍓 \↓/ <🍊> 🍉
```

    import Stack exposing (topAndBelow)

    FocusList.only 0
        |> FocusList.squeezeStackInBefore
            (topAndBelow -5 [ -4 ])
        |> FocusList.squeezeStackInBefore
            (topAndBelow -3 [ -2, -1 ])
        |> FocusList.toStack
    --> topAndBelow -5 [ -4, -3, -2, -1, 0 ]
values.type: Fillable.Empty Possibly.Possibly (Stack.StackFilled item) -> FocusList.ListFocusingHole possiblyOrNever item -> FocusList.ListFocusingHole possiblyOrNever item

values.name: toList
values.comment:
Converts it to a flattened list:

    FocusList.only 456
        |> FocusList.prepend [ 123 ]
        |> FocusList.append [ 789 ]
        |> FocusList.toList
    --> [ 123, 456, 789 ]

Only use this if you need a list in the end.
Otherwise, use [`toStack`](#toStack) to preserve some information about its length.
values.type: FocusList.ListFocusingHole possiblyOrNever_ item -> List.List item

values.name: toStack
values.comment:
Flattens the `FocusList` into a [`Stack`](Stack#StackFilled):

    import Fillable
    import Stack exposing (topAndBelow)

    FocusList.empty
        |> FocusList.toStack
    --> Fillable.empty

    FocusList.only 123
        |> FocusList.append [ 789 ]
        |> FocusList.nextHole
        |> FocusList.plug 456
        |> FocusList.toStack
    --> topAndBelow 123 [ 456, 789 ]

the type information gets carried over, so

    Item -> Stack.Never
    Possibly
values.type: FocusList.ListFocusingHole possiblyOrNever item -> Fillable.Empty possiblyOrNever (Stack.StackFilled item)

name: Stack

aliases.name: StackFilled
aliases.comment:
A non-empty representation of a stack. [`top`](#top), [`removeTop`](#removeTop), [`addOnTop`](#addOnTop) [`mapTop`](#mapTop) are `O(n)`


#### in arguments

[`Empty`](Fillable#Empty) `Never (StackFilled ...)` → stack is non-empty:

    top : Empty Never (StackFilled element) -> element


#### in results

[`Empty`](Fillable#Empty) [`Possibly`](https://dark.elm.dmy.fr/packages/lue-bird/elm-allowable-state/latest/Possibly) `(StackFilled ...)` → stack could be empty

    fromList : List element -> Empty Possibly (StackFilled element)

We can treat it like any [`Fillable.Empty`](Fillable#Empty):

    import Fillable exposing (filled, Empty(..))
    import Possibly exposing (Possibly)
    import Stack exposing (StackFilled, top)

    Fillable.empty : Stack Possibly element_ -- fine

    toList : Empty possiblyOrNever_ (StackFilled element) -> List element
    toList =
        \stack ->
            case stack of
                Filled ( top_, belowTop_ ) ->
                    top_ :: belowTop_

                Empty _ ->
                    []

    [ "hi", "there" ] -- comes in as an argument
        |> Stack.fromList
        |> Fillable.map (filled >> top)
    --: Empty String Possibly

Most operations also allow a different type for the top element → see [`StackWithTop`](#StackWithTop)

aliases.name: StackWithTop
aliases.comment:
[`StackFilled`](#StackFilled) with a different type for the top element:

    top : Empty Never (StackWithTop top belowTopElement_) -> top

it's the result of:

  - [`only`](#only)
  - [`topAndBelow`](#topAndBelow)
  - [`fromTopAndBelow`](#fromTopAndBelow)
  - [`addOnTop`](#addOnTop)
  - [`mapTop`](#mapTop)
  - [`map2TopAndDown`](#map2TopAndDown)

values.name: addOnTop
values.comment:
Add an element to the front.

    import Fillable
    import Stack exposing (topAndBelow, addOnTop)

    topAndBelow 2 [ 3 ] |> addOnTop 1
    --> topAndBelow 1 [ 2, 3 ]

    Fillable.empty |> addOnTop 1
    --> Stack.only 1
values.type: newTop -> Fillable.Empty possiblyOrNever_ (Stack.StackFilled belowElement) -> Fillable.Empty never_ (Stack.StackWithTop newTop belowElement)

values.name: concat
values.comment:
Glue together a bunch of stacks.

    import Fillable
    import Stack exposing (topAndBelow)

    topAndBelow
        (topAndBelow 0 [ 1 ])
        [ topAndBelow 10 [ 11 ]
        , Fillable.empty
        , topAndBelow 20 [ 21, 22 ]
        ]
        |> Stack.concat
    --> topAndBelow 0 [ 1, 10, 11, 20, 21, 22 ]

For this to return a non-empty stack, there must be a non-empty [`top`](#top) stack.
values.type: Fillable.Empty possiblyOrNever (Stack.StackWithTop (Fillable.Empty possiblyOrNever (Stack.StackFilled element)) (Fillable.Empty belowTopListsPossiblyOrNever_ (Stack.StackFilled element))) -> Fillable.Empty possiblyOrNever (Stack.StackFilled element)

values.name: fold
values.comment:
A fold in a [direction](https://package.elm-lang.org/packages/lue-bird/elm-linear-direction/latest/)
where the initial result is the [`top`](#top).

    import LinearDirection exposing (LinearDirection(..))
    import Stack exposing (topAndBelow)

    topAndBelow 234 [ 345, 543 ]
        |> Stack.fold FirstToLast max
    --> 543
values.type: LinearDirection.LinearDirection -> (belowTopElement -> top -> top) -> Fillable.Empty Basics.Never (Stack.StackWithTop top belowTopElement) -> top

values.name: foldFrom
values.comment:
Reduce in a [direction](https://package.elm-lang.org/packages/lue-bird/elm-linear-direction/latest/).

    import LinearDirection exposing (LinearDirection(..))
    import Stack exposing (topAndBelow)

    topAndBelow 'l' [ 'i', 'v', 'e' ]
        |> Stack.foldFrom "" LastToFirst String.cons
    --> "live"

    topAndBelow 'l' [ 'i', 'v', 'e' ]
        |> Stack.foldFrom "" FirstToLast String.cons
    --> "evil"
values.type: accumulationValue -> LinearDirection.LinearDirection -> (element -> accumulationValue -> accumulationValue) -> Fillable.Empty possiblyOrNever_ (Stack.StackFilled element) -> accumulationValue

values.name: fromList
values.comment:
Convert a `List element` to a `Empty Possibly (StackFilled element)`.
The `List`s `head` becomes [`top`](#top), its `tail` is shoved down below.

    import Possibly exposing (Possibly)
    import Fillable
    import Stack exposing (topAndBelow)

    [] |> Stack.fromList
    --> Fillable.empty

    [ "hello", "emptiness" ] |> Stack.fromList
    --> topAndBelow "hello" [ "emptiness" ]
    --: Empty Possibly (StackFilled String)

When constructing from known elements, always prefer

    import Stack exposing (topAndBelow)

    topAndBelow "hello" [ "emptiness" ]
values.type: List.List element -> Fillable.Empty Possibly.Possibly (Stack.StackFilled element)

values.name: fromTopAndBelow
values.comment:
Convert from a tuple `( top, List belowElement )`.

Currently equivalent to [`filled`](Fillable#filled).
values.type: ( top, List.List belowElement ) -> Fillable.Empty never_ (Stack.StackWithTop top belowElement)

values.name: length
values.comment:
How many element there are.

    import Stack exposing (addOnTop)

    Stack.only 3
        |> addOnTop 2
        |> Stack.length
    --> 2
values.type: Fillable.Empty possiblyOrNever_ (Stack.StackWithTop top_ belowElement_) -> Basics.Int

values.name: map
values.comment:
Apply a function to every element.

    import Stack exposing (topAndBelow)

    topAndBelow 1 [ 4, 9 ]
        |> Stack.map negate
    --> topAndBelow -1 [ -4, -9 ]
values.type: (aElement -> bElement) -> Fillable.Empty possiblyOrNever (Stack.StackFilled aElement) -> Fillable.Empty possiblyOrNever (Stack.StackFilled bElement)

values.name: map2
values.comment:
Combine every element in 2 stacks with a given function.
If one stack is longer, its extra elements are dropped.

    import Fillable
    import Stack exposing (topAndBelow)

    Stack.map2 (+)
        (topAndBelow 1 [ 2, 3 ])
        (topAndBelow 4 [ 5, 6, 7 ])
    --> topAndBelow 5 [ 7, 9 ]

    Stack.map2 Tuple.pair
        (topAndBelow 1 [ 2, 3 ])
       Fillable.empty
    --> Fillable.empty

For [`StackWithTop top belowTopElement`](#StackWithTop) where `top` and `belowTopElement` have a different type,
there's [`map2TopAndDown`](#map2TopAndDown).
values.type: (aElement -> bElement -> combinedElement) -> Fillable.Empty possiblyOrNever (Stack.StackFilled aElement) -> Fillable.Empty possiblyOrNever (Stack.StackFilled bElement) -> Fillable.Empty possiblyOrNever (Stack.StackFilled combinedElement)

values.name: map2TopAndDown
values.comment:
Combine the top and below elements of 2 stacks using given functions.
If one stack is longer, its extra elements are dropped.

    import Fillable
    import Stack exposing (topAndBelow, map2TopAndDown)

    map2TopAndDown Tuple.pair (+)
        (topAndBelow "hey" [ 0, 1 ])
        (topAndBelow "there" [ 1, 6, 7 ])
    --> topAndBelow ( "hey", "there" ) [ 1, 7 ]

    map2TopAndDown Tuple.pair (+)
        (topAndBelow 1 [ 2, 3 ])
       Fillable.empty
    --> Fillable.empty

Use [`map2`](#map2) if [`top`](#top) and `belowElement` types match.
values.type: (aTop -> bTop -> topCombined) -> (aBelowElement -> bBelowElement -> belowElementCombined) -> Fillable.Empty possiblyOrNever (Stack.StackWithTop aTop aBelowElement) -> Fillable.Empty possiblyOrNever (Stack.StackWithTop bTop bBelowElement) -> Fillable.Empty possiblyOrNever (Stack.StackWithTop topCombined belowElementCombined)

values.name: mapBelowTop
values.comment:
Apply a function to every element of its removeTop.

    import Stack exposing (topAndBelow, mapBelowTop)

    topAndBelow 1 [ 4, 9 ]
        |> mapBelowTop negate
    --> topAndBelow 1 [ -4, -9 ]
values.type: (belowElement -> belowElementMapped) -> Fillable.Empty possiblyOrNever (Stack.StackWithTop top belowElement) -> Fillable.Empty possiblyOrNever (Stack.StackWithTop top belowElementMapped)

values.name: mapTop
values.comment:
Change the [`top`](#top) element based on its current value.
Its type is allowed to change.

    import Stack exposing (topAndBelow, mapTop)

    topAndBelow 1 [ 4, 9 ]
        |> mapTop negate
    --> topAndBelow -1 [ 4, 9 ]
values.type: (top -> topMapped) -> Fillable.Empty possiblyOrNever (Stack.StackWithTop top belowElement) -> Fillable.Empty possiblyOrNever (Stack.StackWithTop topMapped belowElement)

values.name: only
values.comment:
A stack with just 1 single element.

    import Fillable
    import Stack exposing (addOnTop)

    Stack.only ":)"
    --> Fillable.empty |> addOnTop ":)"
values.type: top -> Fillable.Empty never_ (Stack.StackWithTop top belowTopElement_)

values.name: removeTop
values.comment:
Everything after the first value.

    import Stack exposing (topAndBelow, addOnTop, stackOnTop, removeTop)

    Stack.only 2
        |> addOnTop 3
        |> stackOnTop (topAndBelow 1 [ 0 ])
        |> removeTop
    --> topAndBelow 0 [ 3, 2 ]
    --: Empty Possibly (StackFilled number_)
values.type: Fillable.Empty Basics.Never (Stack.StackWithTop top_ belowElement) -> Fillable.Empty Possibly.Possibly (Stack.StackFilled belowElement)

values.name: reverse
values.comment:
Flip the order of the elements.

    import Stack exposing (topAndBelow)

    topAndBelow "l" [ "i", "v", "e" ]
        |> Stack.reverse
    --> topAndBelow "e" [ "v", "i", "l" ]
values.type: Fillable.Empty possiblyOrNever (Stack.StackFilled element) -> Fillable.Empty possiblyOrNever (Stack.StackFilled element)

values.name: splitTop
values.comment:
Convert to a non-empty list tuple `( top, List belowElement )`.

Currently equivalent to [`filling`](Fillable#filling).

    import Stack exposing (StackFilled, topAndBelow, splitTop)

    topAndBelow "hi" [ "there", "👋" ]
        |> splitTop
    --> ( "hi", topAndBelow "there" [ "👋" ] )
    --: ( String, Empty Possibly (StackFilled String) )
values.type: Fillable.Empty Basics.Never (Stack.StackWithTop top belowElement) -> ( top, Fillable.Empty Possibly.Possibly (Stack.StackFilled belowElement) )

values.name: stackOnTop
values.comment:
Glue the elements of a stack to the end of another stack.

    import Stack exposing (topAndBelow, stackOnTop)

    topAndBelow 1 [ 2 ]
        |> stackOnTop (topAndBelow -1 [ 0 ])
    --> topAndBelow -1 [ 0, 1, 2 ]

Prefer this over [`stackTypedOnTop`](#stackTypedOnTop) if the piped stack is already known as non-empty
or if both can be empty.
values.type: Fillable.Empty appendedPossiblyOrNever_ (Stack.StackFilled element) -> Fillable.Empty possiblyOrNever (Stack.StackFilled element) -> Fillable.Empty possiblyOrNever (Stack.StackFilled element)

values.name: stackTypedOnTop
values.comment:
Glue the elements of an `Empty possiblyOrNever`/`Never` [`Stack`](#StackFilled) to the end of a [`Stack`](#StackFilled).

    import Fillable
    import Stack exposing (topAndBelow, stackOnTop, stackTypedOnTop)

    Fillable.empty
        |> stackTypedOnTop
            (topAndBelow 1 [ 2 ])
        |> stackOnTop
            (topAndBelow -2 [ -1, 0 ])
    --> topAndBelow -2 [ -1, 0, 1, 2 ]

Prefer [`stackOnTop`](#stackOnTop) if the piped stack is already known as non-empty
or if both are `Possibly`.
values.type: Fillable.Empty possiblyOrNever (Stack.StackWithTop newTop element) -> Fillable.Empty inPossiblyOrNever_ (Stack.StackFilled element) -> Fillable.Empty possiblyOrNever (Stack.StackWithTop newTop element)

values.name: toList
values.comment:
Convert it to a `List`.

    import Stack exposing (topAndBelow)

    topAndBelow 1 [ 7 ] |> Stack.toList
    --> [ 1, 7 ]
values.type: Fillable.Empty possiblyOrNever_ (Stack.StackFilled element) -> List.List element

values.name: toTopAndBelow
values.comment:
Convert to a non-empty list tuple `( top, List belowElement )`.

Currently equivalent to [`filling`](Fillable#filling).

    import Stack exposing (topAndBelow, toTopAndBelow)

    topAndBelow "hi" [ "there", "👋" ]
        |> toTopAndBelow
    --> ( "hi", [ "there", "👋" ] )
values.type: Fillable.Empty Basics.Never (Stack.StackWithTop top belowElement) -> ( top, List.List belowElement )

values.name: top
values.comment:
The first value.

    import Stack exposing (top, addOnTop)

    Stack.only 3
        |> addOnTop 2
        |> top
    --> 2
values.type: Fillable.Empty Basics.Never (Stack.StackWithTop top belowTop_) -> top

values.name: topAndBelow
values.comment: A stack from a top element and a `List` of elements below going down.
values.type: top -> List.List belowElement -> Fillable.Empty never_ (Stack.StackWithTop top belowElement)

values.name: when
values.comment:
Keep elements that satisfy a test.

    import Stack exposing (topAndBelow)

    topAndBelow 1 [ 2, 5, -3, 10 ]
        |> Stack.when (\x -> x < 5)
    --> topAndBelow 1 [ 2, -3 ]
    --: Empty Possibly (StackFilled number_)
values.type: (element -> Basics.Bool) -> Fillable.Empty possiblyOrNever_ (Stack.StackFilled element) -> Fillable.Empty Possibly.Possibly (Stack.StackFilled element)

values.name: whenFilled
values.comment:
Keep all [`filled`](Fillable#filled) values and drop all [`empty`](Fillable#empty) elements.

    import Fillable exposing (filled)
    import Stack exposing (topAndBelow)

    topAndBelow Fillable.empty [ Fillable.empty ]
        |> Stack.whenFilled
    --> Fillable.empty

    topAndBelow (filled 1) [ Fillable.empty, filled 3 ]
        |> Stack.whenFilled
    --> topAndBelow 1 [ 3 ]

As you can see, if only the top is [`filling`](Fillable#filling) a value, the result is non-empty.
values.type: Fillable.Empty possiblyOrNever (Stack.StackWithTop (Fillable.Empty possiblyOrNever topValue) (Fillable.Empty belowPossiblyOrNever_ belowElementValue)) -> Fillable.Empty possiblyOrNever (Stack.StackWithTop topValue belowElementValue)
